items.title: Alleged Mongols associate on the run after escaping custody in Darwin

children.content: Police are searching for remand prisoner Isaac Thompson, 26, who they say is a "known associate" of the Mongols outlaw motorcycle gang, after he escaped custody during a medical transfer on Sunday night.

image.altText: White man with dark brown hair, brown eyes, thick brown eyebrows with a moustache and beard.

items.title: 'Oldest continually practised medicine' being passed on to students

children.content: Aboriginal Australians have used native plants as bush medicine for tens of thousands of years. On Larrakia land, medical students are learning how nature can heal.

image.altText: A man wearing a brown hat takes a bite of a small passionfruit.

items.title: New Darwin service provides parents with mental health support

children.content: Natalya Wallace thought she just had the baby blues as a result of a traumatic birth experience. When she realised she needed mental health support, the young mother was relieved to find a new service in her rural area.

gallery.altText: A woman with long brown hair wearing a blue and white checked top smiles at the camera.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with long brown hair wearing a blue and white checked top smiles at the camera.

avatar.alt: Jane Bardon in a blazer looking at the camera.

items.title: Locals worried remote Gove Peninsular town won't survive housing crisis

children.content: A government-funded housing provider in the Northern Territory mining town of Nhulunbuy has been criticised for "not doing enough" to support small business owners.

image.altText: Tradespeople wearing high-vis uniforms working on a construction site in a regional area.

items.title: Prosperous NT mining town prepares for tough times as Rio Tinto exit looms

children.content: In Australia's tropical north, a mining town is preparing for the departure of its biggest employer, and locals are accusing the mining giant of squeezing out small businesses on its way out.

image.altText: Streets of homes and two large green ovals can be seen in an aerial shot of a remote coastal community.

items.title: Chilling image from January 26 that stops gallery-goers in their tracks

children.content: There is a wide range of artworks among the finalists vying for this year's prestigious National Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Art Awards, from photography and painting to multimedia and sculpture.

gallery.altText: A black-and-white photo of a smiling Aboriginal girl on her dad's shoulders as they march through the city.

thumbnail.altText: A black-and-white photo of a smiling Aboriginal girl on her dad's shoulders as they march through the city.

tags.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Art

items.title: The desert kids making classic hard rock cool again

children.content: Inspired by AC/DC and raised in a remote NT community, these young cousins have become one of Australia's most unlikely hard rock bands.

image.altText: Six members of Mulga Bore Hard Rock with black-and-white face paint pose for a group portrait in a studio setting

items.title: How NAIDOC grew from a one-day protest to a week-long celebration

children.content: Traditionally, NAIDOC Week was celebrated by First Nations people, but it has become an invitation for non-Indigenous Australians to get involved.

image.altText: A montage of images of NAIDOC protests and image of Day of Mourning with Indigenous leaders in 1938

Harry Kane and Jude Bellingham celebrate an England goal
Jarell Quansah's red card challenge on Jesus Gallardo

articles.title: Ice-cold Haaland triumphs again at World Cup, Brazil with a long road back

children.content: Brazil has long been trying to re-capture its glory days, but they are fading further into obscurity, while Erling Haaland proves Norway is a genuine World Cup threat in 2-1 win.

image.altText: Erling Haaland looks tough in celebration of a goal

articles.title: Meet Mr Row Row, the man who invented Norway's viral World Cup Viking row

children.content: Ole Frøystad has always been a passionate football fan and when Norway qualified for its first men's World Cup since 1998, he was inspired to create the iconic chant that's become a global sensation.

image.altText: Norway fans sitting in Times Square doing a Viking row

articles.title: Belgium slams FIFA decision after Trump intervenes on US star's behalf

children.content: US striker Folarin Balogun is free to play Belgium despite earning a red card in his last match, in a shock decision that delights Donald Trump and "astonishes" the Belgians.

image.altText: Folarin Balogun points to his forehead in celebration

children.content: Ten-man England holds on to defeat Mexico 3-2 in a World Cup classic at the Azteca, with Jude Bellingham's brace and Harry Kane's penalty enough to send the Three Lions through. Follow live.

items.title: The world is getting pure Trump and the US presidency may never be the same

children.content: Donald Trump is undertaking regime change, not just in Venezuela, but on US soil and it is unlikely the presidency will ever be the same.

items.title: Like 'playing chess': The yellow blocks marking a new 'game' in Gaza

children.content: The "Yellow Line" that marks the boundary of Israeli-controlled territory in Gaza has been moved deeper into the enclave.

items.title: Reporting on Venezuela's disaster when we heard: 'Call your embassy right now'

children.content: Instead of focusing all its efforts on coordinating a widescale rescue operation, the Venezuelan government has been accused of hindering the response, and as has become apparent, our ability to cover it.

children.content: A record number of exchange traded funds were added to the Australian share market in the past financial year.

children.content: Australia's Space Agency says objects found on a north Queensland beach are likely pressure vessels from a space launch vehicle.

items.title: NSW government announces review into boy's death amid cannibalism probe

children.content: Cannibalism will form part of an investigation into the death of a four-year-old boy on the NSW Central Coast, with an independent review to take place by the Department of Communities and Justice.

children.content: As part of NAIDOC Week, we meet the Indigenous creatives who are re-shaping Australia's video game industry.

items.title: Is a full day of screen time for kids ever OK?

children.content: Screen time has become a phrase that makes "parents feel instantly guilty", but how detrimental is a full day of screens on the odd occasion, like during school holidays or when you are home sick?

items.title: History quiz: Tackle our questions on Harry Potter, space stations and more

children.content: Let us test your smarts about Captain James Cook's last voyage, the debut release of a popular British girl group and other historical events.

image.altText: Harry Potter in his cloak with his wand out, in front of an eerie smoky green and black background.

items.title: Neil the Seal is 1 tonne right now. What happens when he's 4 tonnes?

children.content: The elephant seal has been attracting onlookers in southern Tasmania as well as a growing social media audience. But fears for his wellbeing, and concerns that some people aren't keeping their distance, have led to calls to better protect Neil.

gallery.altText: An elephant seal known as Neil the Seal lies on a beach with people watching him from a safe distance.

thumbnail.altText: An elephant seal known as Neil the Seal lies on a beach with people watching him from a safe distance.

items.title: Nigel Farage referred to watchdog over 'gifts' from crypto investor 'Posh George'

children.content: Nigel Farage was reportedly provided with security services, social media support and accommodation by George Cottrell, a 32-year-old crypto investor who previously served eight months in prison for wire fraud.

image.altText: Nigel Farage smiles as he drinks a beer while walking next to a young man in a suit.

items.title: China to test ballistic missile in Pacific — as it happened

children.content: The Chinese government informed governments in the region about the test earlier today, the ABC has been told.

image.altText: A hypersonic missile blasts off from land.

items.title: ASX retreats amid takeover battle for miner as oil price falls

children.content: The ASX is down in afternoon trade, despite the Gulf ceasefire holding and a positive sentiment on global markets. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: By-election to follow minister's resignation amid One Nation surge

children.content: WA Labor stalwart and senior state cabinet minister Paul Papalia has announced he is quitting politics after nearly two decades, triggering a by-election in his southern Perth seat.

image.altText: A close up of Paul Papalia who wears a black suit as he holds a press conference

items.title: Whale freed from fishing line in 'unprecedented' entanglement spike

children.content: Whale rescuers say there have been 24 entanglements already this migration season. Meanwhile, a small whale was euthanised after it stranded itself on a beach near Newcastle, despite rescue efforts from residents and experts.

items.title: First Nations community taking cancer care into its own hands

children.content: A new program in Far North Queensland aims to make life easier for people receiving treatment for cancer, while reducing the number of people who develop the disease.

image.altText: A man wearing a bright blue fishing shirt smiles at the camera. He is standing on a tropical beach.

items.title: AFP to push Australia's policing model to UN

children.content: The AFP commissioner will promote Pacific police training as a model for the United Nations when she addresses police delegates from 140 nations this week.

image.altText: Krissy Barrett

items.title: Experts and union warn about ABC's AI use

children.content: Experts and the journalists’ union have welcomed the ABC’s plans to use artificial intelligence responsibly, but warned misuse could damage trust in the public broadcaster.

image.altText: An outdoor sign displays the ABC logo and the text "ABC" on a brown background

items.title: First Nations commissioner warns against Queensland adoption push

children.content: Australia's Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander children commissioner has warned Queensland's push to increase adoptions could disproportionately affect First Nations children and risk repeating the harms of past forced child removals.

image.altText: Yoorrook Commissioner Sue-Anne Hunter standing against a hand rail

items.title: More than 80 bar staff face uncertain future as seven Hobart venues close

children.content: Seven Hobart bars, including the Observatory Bar and the Republic, are closing "in the immediate term" as owner Pub Banc Group is placed into voluntary administration. The administrator says the aim is for the venues to reopen under new ownership.

image.altText: A composite image of the signs of the Republic, Cargo and Observatory bars.

items.title: Sabalenka to 'get drunk and forget about tennis' after Wimbledon exit

children.content: Naomi Osaka downs top seed Aryna Sabalenka in straight sets in a fourth round meeting of grand slam champions at Wimbledon.

items.title: Munster ready to 'run through a brick wall' for Maroons in Origin decider

children.content: Cameron Munster wants to honour Queensland State of Origin greats by leading the Maroons to a series victory over NSW in Brisbane on Wednesday night.

items.title: Pogačar hits back at rival as teammate wins stage two of Tour de France

items.title: Wildcards from Whyalla return to win grand prix after earlier heartbreak

children.content: A year after a devastating technical disqualification snatched victory from five South Australian teens at a hydrogen-powered engineering race, they returned as wildcard entrants to the 2026 H2 Grand Prix Finals in Switzerland.
